christmas.summary: Christmas is an annual festival commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ, observed primarily on December 25 as a religious and cultural celebration among billions of people around the world.
christmas.history: Modern Christmas traditions evolved from various religious and secular origins, including the Nativity of Jesus and winter solstice festivals. It became a public holiday in many countries in the 19th century.
christmas.why_people_search: People search for the Christmas countdown to plan holiday gatherings, travel, shopping, and to build excitement for one of the biggest festive seasons of the year.

new-year.summary: New Year's Day is the first day of the year on the modern Gregorian calendar as well as the Julian calendar.
new-year.history: In many countries, New Year's Day is celebrated with fireworks and other festivities at midnight as the new year begins in each time zone. It is a time for resolutions and new beginnings.
new-year.why_people_search: People search for the New Year countdown to experience the exact moment of the transition into the next year, often as part of parties or midnight celebrations.

valentines-day.summary: Valentine's Day, also called Saint Valentine's Day or the Feast of Saint Valentine, is celebrated annually on February 14.
valentines-day.history: Originating as a Christian feast day honoring one or two early Christian martyrs named Saint Valentine, it has become a significant cultural celebration of romance and love.
valentines-day.why_people_search: Countdowns to Valentine's Day are popular for planning romantic dinners, gift purchases, and ensuring special plans are made in time.

halloween.summary: Halloween or Hallowe'en is a celebration observed in many countries on 31 October, the eve of the Western Christian feast of All Hallows' Day.
halloween.history: It is widely believed that many Halloween traditions originated from ancient Celtic harvest festivals, particularly the Gaelic festival Samhain.
halloween.why_people_search: Fans of spooky season search for this countdown to prepare costumes, decorate their homes, and plan themed parties well in advance.

earth-day.summary: Earth Day is an annual event on April 22 to demonstrate support for environmental protection.
earth-day.history: First held on April 22, 1970, it now includes a wide range of events coordinated globally by Earthday.org including 1 billion people in more than 193 countries.
earth-day.why_people_search: Schools, organizations, and individuals use the countdown to coordinate environmental awareness campaigns and local cleanup events.

summer.summary: Summer is the warmest of the four temperate seasons, falling between spring and autumn.
summer.history: Astronomically, the summer solstice (around June 21 in the Northern Hemisphere) marks the official start of summer, characterized by the longest day of the year.
summer.why_people_search: The countdown to summer is a major trend for students looking forward to school breaks and travelers planning their summer vacations.

winter.summary: Winter is the coldest season of the year in polar and temperate climates, between autumn and spring.
winter.history: Winter begins at the winter solstice, which is the shortest day of the year. In the Northern Hemisphere, this usually occurs around December 21-22.
winter.why_people_search: People search for the winter countdown to prepare for colder weather, winter sports, and the distinctive cozy atmosphere of the season.

spring.summary: Spring is the season of new beginnings. It is the time when flowers bloom and trees start to grow.
spring.history: Spring's arrival is marked by the spring equinox (around March 20-21), when day and night are of approximately equal length.
spring.why_people_search: Gardeners and nature lovers track the spring countdown to know when to start planting and to anticipate the return of warmer, brighter days.

autumn.summary: Autumn, also known as fall, is the season when the duration of daylight becomes noticeably shorter and the temperature cools considerably.
autumn.history: Autumn begins with the autumnal equinox (around September 22-23). It is traditionally associated with the harvest season in many cultures.
autumn.why_people_search: People look forward to autumn for the changing colors of leaves, 'pumpkin spice' culture, and the relief from summer heat.

ramadan.summary: Ramadan is the ninth month of the Islamic calendar, observed by Muslims worldwide as a month of fasting, prayer, and reflection.
ramadan.history: The month commemorates the first revelation of the Quran to Prophet Muhammad. It is one of the Five Pillars of Islam.
ramadan.why_people_search: Because the Islamic calendar is lunar, the dates of Ramadan change every year. People search for the countdown to prepare for fasting and religious observances.

eid-al-fitr.summary: Eid al-Fitr is the 'Festival of Breaking the Fast', a religious holiday celebrated by Muslims worldwide that marks the end of the month-long dawn-to-sunset fasting of Ramadan.
eid-al-fitr.history: It was originated by the Islamic prophet Muhammad. Certain traditions say these festivals were initiated in Medina after the migration of Muhammad from Mecca.
eid-al-fitr.why_people_search: As the date depends on the sighting of the moon, the countdown helps the community prepare for the grand three-day celebration.

eid-al-adha.summary: Eid al-Adha or the Feast of Sacrifice is the second of the two main Islamic holidays celebrated worldwide each year.
eid-al-adha.history: It honors the willingness of Ibrahim to sacrifice his son Ismael as an act of obedience to God's command.
eid-al-adha.why_people_search: People track this countdown to plan for the Hajj pilgrimage season and the traditional sharing of food with the community.

easter.summary: Easter, also called Pascha or Resurrection Sunday, is a Christian festival and cultural holiday commemorating the resurrection of Jesus from the dead.
easter.history: The date of Easter is variable and is calculated based on the first full moon after the vernal equinox.
easter.why_people_search: Since the date changes annually, families and travelers need the countdown to schedule school holidays and traditional Easter egg hunts.

black-friday.summary: Black Friday is a colloquial term for the Friday following Thanksgiving Day in the United States, which has been regarded as the beginning of the Christmas shopping season.
black-friday.history: Historically, it was the day retailers traditionally moved from 'the red' (losses) to 'the black' (profit).
black-friday.why_people_search: Shoppers search for this countdown to stay updated on early deals, store opening times, and online sales events.

cyber-monday.summary: Cyber Monday is a marketing term for the Monday after the Thanksgiving holiday in the United States, encouraging people to shop online.
cyber-monday.history: It was created by marketing companies to encourage people to shop online, especially after the physical shopping frenzy of Black Friday.
cyber-monday.why_people_search: Digital consumers track this countdown to access exclusive online-only discounts and tech deals.

thanksgiving.summary: Thanksgiving is a national holiday in the United States, celebrated on the fourth Thursday of November.
thanksgiving.history: It began as a day of giving thanks for the blessing of the harvest and of the preceding year.
thanksgiving.why_people_search: Families use the countdown to coordinate travel, host dinner parties, and prepare for the traditional turkey feast.

diwali.summary: Diwali is the Hindu festival of lights with its variations also celebrated in other Indian religions. It symbolizes the spiritual 'victory of light over darkness'.
diwali.history: Deepavali is a festival that lasts for five days and is celebrated during the Hindu lunisolar month Kartika. It is one of the most popular festivals of Hinduism.
diwali.why_people_search: The dates of Diwali vary annually; the countdown is essential for planning home decorations, firework displays, and gift exchanges.

chinese-new-year.summary: Chinese New Year, also known as the Lunar New Year or Spring Festival, is the festival that celebrates the beginning of a new year on the traditional lunisolar Chinese calendar.
chinese-new-year.history: It is one of the most important holidays in China and among Chinese communities worldwide, with traditions like red envelopes and dragon dances.
chinese-new-year.why_people_search: As it follows the lunar calendar, the date changes every year. People search for the countdown to plan family reunions and cultural celebrations.

mothers-day.summary: Mother's Day is a celebration honoring the mother of the family or individual, as well as motherhood, maternal bonds, and the influence of mothers in society.
mothers-day.history: The modern holiday was first celebrated in 1907 when Anna Jarvis held a memorial for her mother at St Andrew's Methodist Church in Grafton, West Virginia.
mothers-day.why_people_search: People search for the countdown to ensure they have enough time to buy gifts, send cards, and arrange special treats for their mothers.

fathers-day.summary: Father's Day is a holiday of honoring fatherhood and paternal bonds, as well as the influence of fathers in society.
fathers-day.history: It was first proposed in the early 20th century to complement Mother's Day, becoming an official US holiday in 1972.
fathers-day.why_people_search: Like Mother's Day, the countdown helps children and spouses plan celebrations and gifts for the fathers in their lives.
